sketch: VaultCore's surface splits gallery state into two types: `LockedGallery` (Copyable, Sendable, ciphertext-only — can enumerate chunks, verify AEAD tags, and serve sync) and a move-only `UnlockSession` (~Copyable) returned by `try lockedGallery.unlock(password:)`, which runs Argon2id, unwraps the DEK straight into a sodium_malloc/sodium_mlock guarded allocation, and never materializes it as `Data`. `lock()` is a `consuming func` that sodium_memzeros and frees the DEK, and deinit does the same, so use-after-lock is a compile error and forgotten sessions still self-zeroize; there is no way to copy the session into a cache or closure by accident. Reads go through borrowing child handles (`FileReader`, non-Sendable, lifetime-bound to the session via ~Escapable/borrowing parameters) whose only plaintext exit is `withDecryptedChunk(range:) { (buf: borrowing SecureBytes) in ... }` — SecureBytes is itself ~Copyable, backed by sodium_malloc with guard pages, zeroed on deinit. Streaming playback declares a resident-plaintext budget at unlock time (`unlock(password:, budget: .bytes(32 << 20))`); the session's internal allocator refuses to hold more concurrently-live decrypted chunks than the budget, which is exactly the accounting the AVAssetResourceLoader leg needs. Because the CRDT sync engine and CLI only ever need `LockedGallery`, 'sync never sees plaintext' becomes a type-level fact rather than a code-review claim, directly discharging green gate 3 and most of spec §11's memory-hygiene checklist at the API boundary instead of at every call site. The whole scheme is UIKit-free and lands cleanly in Workstream A as the package's public face over Workstreams B/C.
risk: Swift's noncopyable/lifetime feature set is the load-bearing bet and it is still jagged: ~Copyable types cannot live in most generic containers, existentials, or escaping/async closures, and the real streaming consumer (AVAssetResourceLoaderDelegate) is a cross-thread callback API — if non-Sendable borrowing handles can't survive that boundary, every escape hatch added (an actor wrapper, an @unchecked Sendable box) quietly converts the compile-time guarantees back into runtime discipline. Secondary but real: `withBytes` closures can still copy bytes out into a `Data`, so the custody story must be honest that it narrows the plaintext surface rather than eliminating it.
first_step: Implement `SecureBytes` first: a ~Copyable struct owning a sodium_malloc'd region with mlock, a borrowing `withUnsafeBytes` accessor, and sodium_memzero+sodium_free in deinit — plus two tests: a runtime test using a debug deallocation hook asserting the region is zeroed before free, and a compile-fail harness (SwiftPM test target that shells `swiftc` over misuse snippets, asserting non-compilation) proving copy, escape, and use-after-consume are rejected. Everything else in the design composes on top of this one type.
children: Actor facade for the playback leg: a `StreamingSessionActor` that owns the UnlockSession internally (move-only inside, Sendable async chunk API outside) so AVAssetResourceLoader callbacks get `func decryptedRange(_:) async throws` with budget enforcement — the escape hatch is one audited file instead of scattered @unchecked Sendable.

Compile-fail tests as a green gate: generalize the swiftc-negative-compilation harness into a 'misuse matrix' (use-after-lock, session capture in @Sendable closure, SecureBytes copy, handle outliving session) so the type-state guarantees are regression-tested like behavior, not just asserted in docs.

Ciphertext-only capability for sync: give LockedGallery a `SyncView` protocol exposing chunk enumeration, existence checks, and AEAD verification with no unlock path at all — the future CRDT engine and BlobStore reconciler compile against SyncView, making zero-knowledge sync structurally checkable.

Budget tied to platform memory pressure: make the resident-plaintext budget dynamic — a token bucket that shrinks on os memory-pressure notifications (or a fixed cap on Linux CLI), so scrubbing video degrades cache depth instead of getting the app jetsammed with plaintext resident.

Auto-lock as lease renewal: model backgrounding/timeout by making the session a lease — internal DEK guard flips to invalidated on scenePhase-background or timer expiry (runtime check on every withDecryptedChunk), while explicit lock() stays consuming; this covers spec §11's zero-on-backgrounding without breaking the type-state story.
